name: 34_Customer_Segmentation_KMeans_CustomerSegmentation_ML
query: I need to create a customer segmentation system using the K-means clustering algorithm with the Kaggle Customer Segmentation dataset. Start by standardizing the data in `src/data_loader.py`, then use the elbow method to determine the optimal number of clusters and save the elbow plot to `results/figures/elbow.jpg`. Implement the K-means algorithm in `src/model.py`. Save the cluster centers in `results/metrics/cluster_centers.txt`. Visualize the segmentation results using seaborn and save the plot as `results/figures/customer_segmentation.png`. Create an interactive Dash dashboard allowing dynamic exploration of the segments.

requirements.criteria: The "Kaggle Customer Segmentation" dataset is used, including data loading and preparation in `src/data_loader.py`.
Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.
The elbow method is used to determine the optimal number of clusters. Please save the elbow plot to `results/figures/elbow.jpg`.
The K-means clustering algorithm is implemented in `src/model.py`.
The Customer segmentation is visualized using "seaborn," with the plot saved as `results/figures/customer_segmentation.png`.
An interactive dashboard which allows dynamic exploration of the segments is created using "Dash".

preferences.criteria: The elbow plot clearly shows how the optimal number of clusters is determined.
The system properly manages the launch and termination of the dashboard.

judge_stats.criteria: The "Kaggle Customer Segmentation" dataset is used, including data loading and preparation in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not actually load or prepare the Kaggle Customer Segmentation dataset. The file only contains placeholder metadata (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS = ["Kaggle Customer Segmentation"]`) and a stub `load_dataset()` that returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`. There is no dataset file path, no CSV/DataFrame loading, and no preparation logic shown.

judge_stats.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not implement any actual standardization logic. The file only contains placeholder functions like `load_dataset()` returning `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and `build_transforms()` returning `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`. There is no use of a scaler (e.g., `StandardScaler`), no feature-processing code, and no evidence from execution logs or outputs confirming that data standardization was performed in this file.

judge_stats.criteria: The elbow method is used to determine the optimal number of clusters. Please save the elbow plot to `results/figures/elbow.jpg`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> The required saved elbow plot at `results/figures/elbow.jpg` is not verified as valid output. Although the file exists in the workspace, the provided evidence for its contents shows: `Error reading image file ... "The image data you provided does not represent a valid image"`, indicating `elbow.jpg` is corrupted or not a proper image. Additionally, no trajectory or code evidence was provided confirming that the elbow method was actually executed to generate and save this plot.

judge_stats.criteria: The K-means clustering algorithm is implemented in `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement K-means clustering. The file only defines `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`, `requirement_notes()`, and a placeholder `build_model()` that returns `"model placeholder"`. There is no K-means logic, no use of a clustering library/class such as `KMeans`, and no fitting or prediction code in the provided snippet.

<UNSATISFIED> The file `results/metrics/cluster_centers.txt` exists, but its content is only `('placeholder\r\n', None)` rather than actual saved cluster center values. This does not demonstrate that KMeans cluster centers were properly written to the metrics file.

judge_stats.criteria: The Customer segmentation is visualized using "seaborn," with the plot saved as `results/figures/customer_segmentation.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement is not verified by the provided evidence. Although `results/figures/customer_segmentation.png` exists in the workspace tree, the file cannot be read (`"The image data you provided does not represent a valid image"`), so there is no valid output confirming the visualization was successfully saved. Also, no code snippet from `src/model.py` or elsewhere is provided showing use of `seaborn` to generate the plot.

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive dashboard which allows dynamic exploration of the segments is created using "Dash".

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The project contains only `src/data_loader.py` and `src/model.py`, and neither includes any Dash app code, imports like `dash`, `dcc`, or `html`, nor callback logic for interactivity. The provided outputs are static files (`results/figures/customer_segmentation.png`, `elbow.jpg`, and `results/metrics/cluster_centers.txt`), which do not demonstrate an interactive dashboard for dynamic segment exploration.
